profile.mfaDescription: Secure your account by enabling MFA. When enabled, you will need to use TOTP codes generated by your app in addition to your username and password. We recommend enabling MFA.
profile.e2eDescription: Enabling this feature will encrypt all communication from/to your remote devices using your own custom encryption keys. Next to our out of the box encryption, this is an additional encryption layer you might activate (so double encryption). Once enabled, livestreams, ptz, io triggers, heartbeats are all end-to-end encrypted.

profile.e2eKeysWarning: Your private key and optional 4-character passphrase will not be stored, so make sure you have copied them. Once you close this modal it is gone forever, and you/we are not able to recover it!

new.k1: Before you will see your recordings shown up on Kerberos Hub, you first need to install and configure a Kerberos Agent. A Kerberos Agent is a piece of software that processes a device, and will make recordings when a portion of the video stream has changed. These recordings will then be send to, and processed by the Kerberos Hub application.
new.k2: After you've properly installed one or more Kerberos Agents, you can link it to the Kerberos Hub application using your
new.k4: . When linked successfully, your device will show up on the devices page.
new.k6: We have different ways to get your Kerberos Agent up and running. You can install the Kerberos Agent using a prebuilt Docker container, inside your Kubernetes Cluster, on your Raspberry Pi (or any other compatible board) or on a simple VM. Please select the most appropriate solution to get started. Visit

end2end.k2: You're now all set, make sure to keep your token generator secure. From now on, you'll need to provide a one-time password on every login attempt.
end2end.k4: This application is using a bi-directional message broker (MQTT) to communicate with your remote devices. This allows you to initiate actions like receiving a livestream, pan-tilting your device and more, without enabling port-forwarding or modifying the network. By default the message broker which is orchestrating these actions is protected with credentials (username and password) and hub encryption, however you might want more security: encryption using your own encryption keys.
end2end.k6: Encryption is a method of secure communication that prevents third-parties from accessing data while it's transferred from one end system or device to another. In this case, the end systems are your devices and your browser. By activating encryption you'll receive a passphrase-protected public key and private key (RSA) which are used to encrypt and decrypt the data you're sending through the MQTT broker, making it unreadable for anyone else other than you.
end2end.k8: You can generate your own keypair using
end2end.k10: or we can generate a public key and private key for you. If you wish to generate one
end2end.k13: By providing a 4-character passphrase, your public key will be encrypted using it. On login you'll be prompted for the same 4-character passphrase to decrypt your public key. Leave the field empty if you don't want to use one.
end2end.k15: Optional. If used, it must be exactly 4 characters.
end2end.k17: Great news, encryption is now enabled. Make sure to securely copy and persist your private key and fingerprint. You'll need to add these credentials in your device deployment to allow encryption.
end2end.k18: As mentioned above only the fingerprint and encrypted public key will be stored in this application. We do not store your private key or optional 4-character passphrase, so keep them somewhere secure.

generate_access_token.k11: At least one scope is required.
generate_access_token.k14: Copy this token now. You won't be able to see it again!
generate_access_token.k15: Token copied to clipboard! Go ahead and leverage
generate_access_token.k17: , insert the token in the Authorization header as a Bearer token.

mfa.k2: Make sure to keep your token generator secure. From now on, you'll need to provide a one-time password on every login attempt.
mfa.k3: Multi-factor authentication (MFA) adds an extra layer of security to your business by addressing the vulnerabilities of relying solely on passwords.
mfa.k5: When enabling MFA you will be asked to enter a time-based one-time password, after signing in with your password. This one-time password is generated by an authenticator app on a device you own. After opening the authenticator of choice, add a new account and the QR code below.
mfa.k7: Once registered, your authenticator app will begin generating unique tokens. If you can’t scan the QR code, you can manually enter the secret key provided instead.
mfa.k8: Enter the verification code generated by your authenticator app

profile.k4: Are you sure you want to disable MFA? This will make your account more vulnerable, as it will only be protected by your password and not by a one-time password.
profile.k7: Are you sure you want to disable encryption? This will make your account more vulnerable, as it will not be protected by encryption.
profile.k9: Are you sure you want to delete this access token?
profile.k44: The URL that will be triggered when HLS generation is done.
profile.k45: You have enabled this feature, and therefore will encrypt all communication from and to your remote devices. For example livestream, ptz and more.
profile.k54: Access tokens allow you to authenticate
profile.k56: requests. They can be created, updated, and deleted as needed. Access tokens provide the foundation for integrating custom applications
profile.k58: . Keep your tokens secure and do not share them with unauthorized parties.
profile.k59: The access token are used as a Bearer token in the Authorization header of your API requests on behalf of the owner user. The token will take into account the expiration and scope levels, which makes sure you grant the least privilege access.

plans.k1: Get the most out of Kerberos Hub
plans.k2: Kerberos Hub supercharges your experience with features like advanced filtering, analysis and parallel livestreaming of all your devices.
plans.k5: The limit you are allowed to upload. Once you reach this limit your account will be disabled during a specific timeout. For example, having a 500 MB daily threshold with a timeout of 6 hours, means your account will be disabled once it reaches the 500 MB upload. After 6 hours your account will be activated again, and you can consume another 500 MB until the limit is reached again. This means that you get by max 4 resets per day; a capacity of max 2 GB each day.
plans.k7: Once your daily threshold is reached, you're account will be disabled, and reset after a specific timeout.

By accessing or using UUG.AI ("Service"), you agree to comply with and be bound by these Terms of Use ("Terms"). If you do not agree with these Terms, you must not use the Service. --- ## 1. **Acceptance of Terms** These Terms constitute a legal agreement between you ("User," "you") and UUG.AI ("Company," "we," "us," "our"). If you are using the Service on behalf of an organization, you represent that you have the authority to bind that organization to these Terms. ## 3. **Account Registration & Security** - You must provide accurate and complete information when creating an account. - You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your login credentials. - Any activity occurring under your account is your responsibility. Notify us immediately of unauthorized access. ## 4. **Use of the Service** - You agree to use the Service only for lawful purposes and in compliance with all applicable laws and regulations. - You shall not misuse the Service, including but not limited to unauthorized access, reverse engineering, or interfering with its operation. - The Service may not be used to infringe on the privacy or rights of others. ## 5. **Data & Privacy** - UUG.AI collects and processes data as described in our **Privacy Policy**. - You are responsible for obtaining consent from individuals being recorded, as required by applicable laws. - We do not claim ownership of the content you upload, but you grant us a limited license to use it for providing the Service. ## 6. **Subscription & Payment** - The Service is provided on a subscription basis, with fees as detailed in our pricing plans. - We may suspend or terminate your access if you violate these Terms. - You may terminate your account at any time, but no refunds will be provided for unused portions of your subscription. ## 10. **Disclaimers & Limitation of Liability** - The Service is provided "as is" without warranties of any kind. - We are not responsible for any damages, including lost data or unauthorized access. - Our total liability shall not exceed the amount paid by you in the past 12 months. ## 11. **Governing Law & Dispute Resolution** -
